behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-API-adapter handoff (MOCK-MISS), not model judgment. A real failure is missing labels in the outbound payload when modules are wired together. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: a local stub transport that captures request body is sufficient; no OS/network/subprocess behavior is required. LLM-DEP demotion proof: behavior text checks plumbing only — "request sent ... includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels"; it does not evaluate classification quality. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior does not require live HTTP conditions; rejected Agentic because assertion is request-shape only, not output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake in-process API client that records outgoing payload; invoke classify() and assert payload contains every taxonomy label exactly once in the sent request.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at response-handoff boundary: provider response parsing + taxonomy guard (MOCK-MISS). A pure unit test of mapper helps, but real regression often comes from adapter parsing plus validator wiring. REAL-INFRA: no real network required; stubbed API response reproduces failure. IA-UA demotion proof: assertion is guardrail plumbing from behavior text — "returns \"unknown\" instead of forwarding" for out-of-taxonomy value — not model quality. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior does not require model correctness, only post-processing rule enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process fake API returning an invalid label string; assert module output is exactly "unknown" and never the raw unexpected label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to one local normalization step with identical input/output determinism. No module interaction, I/O, or model decision required. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text is "normalization step" and "identical raw API responses"; failure boundary is within one function/object.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixtures (case/whitespace variants already fixed per fixture) and assert stable identical final label every run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires "repeated live API calls" and checks consistency of returned labels; correctness depends on real model/provider output behavior (LLM-DEP). Mocking would hide nondeterministic or drift failures (MOCK-HIDE). Rejection gate: rejected Integration because stubbed responses cannot reveal live consistency issues; rejected Workflow because this is a single classification step repeated, not multi-agent chained decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real provider calls for fixed document+taxonomy, record provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label changes only when raw provider response changes; flag unexplained label variance.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local orchestration: token counting/policy + request builder handoff before send (MOCK-MISS). REAL-INFRA: no live provider required because check is explicitly "before dispatch." IA-UA demotion proof: behavior text is plumbing-only — "before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy)" — not classification quality. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic since no dependence on model output content; rejected System since no real network/OS behavior needed.
behaviors.test_description: Wire module with real tokenizer/config plus fake API client; submit over-budget input and assert policy action (truncate or reject) occurs and dispatched payload/input length obeys configured budget.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real provider call and provider-side token accounting; mocks can pass while real model/provider accounting exceeds budget (MOCK-HIDE). By rule, execution through real AI API defaults to Agentic unless only plumbing is tested; here assertion depends on provider/model-produced usage metadata, not just routing. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because stub usage cannot expose provider accounting mismatch; rejected System as minimum because the critical risk is AI-provider tokenization/accounting behavior tied to model call semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification on representative documents, capture provider-reported usage tokens, and assert reported per-call usage <= configured budget across runs/environments.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is retry orchestration across API client + backoff/wait logic + caller contract (module boundary interaction). A deterministic fake API returning rate-limit then success can reproduce it. REAL-INFRA: no true network/subprocess required for minimum detection. IA-UA demotion proof: behavior text checks control-flow plumbing — "waits and retries rather than returning an error" — not model output quality. Rejection gate: rejected System because real HTTP timing is not necessary to catch incorrect retry path; rejected Agentic because label/content quality is irrelevant.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API sequence (rate-limit signal, then success) with controllable clock/sleeper; assert module performs wait/backoff and retries, eventually returning success instead of surfacing immediate error.
